title: Great Chicago Fire | Fleeing the Flames | 2
summary_one_sentence: The Great Chicago Fire of 1871 jumps the river and threatens to destroy downtown and the North Side as panicked residents flee.

title: Hera: Queen of the Gods
summary_one_sentence: Historian Dr Ellie Mackin Roberts joins Tristan Hughes to explore the true origins, worship, and legacy of Hera beyond her jealous-wife stereotype.

title: The Sumerians
summary_one_sentence: Assyriologist Dr Paul Collins joins Tristan Hughes to explore the cities, language, and artifacts of the Sumerian civilization of ancient Iraq.

title: Dead Sea Scrolls: The Copper Scroll
summary_one_sentence: Tristan Hughes visits Jordan to examine the unique metal Copper Scroll and interviews Prof Joan Taylor about its mysterious treasure map contents.

title: The Pinkerton Detective Agency | Brothers and Sons | 2
summary_one_sentence: After founder Allan Pinkerton's death, his sons transform the Pinkerton Detective Agency into feared labor spies and strike-busters, attracting federal scrutiny.

title: Hannibal's March on Rome
summary_one_sentence: Dr Louis Rawlings joins Tristan Hughes to examine Hannibal's 211 BC gamble of marching on Rome and why Carthage ultimately failed despite devastating victories.

title: Prehistoric Japan
summary_one_sentence: Archaeologist Dr Simon Kaner joins Tristan Hughes to explore Japan's prehistoric past from earliest human arrivals to the Jomon culture's hunter-gatherer society.
notes: Jomon culture predates rice farming and is prehistoric human civilization, not paleontology; classified as ancient_history.

title: The Franks
summary_one_sentence: Dr Ian Wood joins Tristan Hughes to trace the Franks' rise from obscure origins to dominance over post-Roman western Europe under kings like Clovis.
notes: Franks are early medieval (post-Roman), placed in ancient_history as the show covers late antiquity/early medieval crossover.

title: The Kingdom of Kush
summary_one_sentence: Dr Loretta Kilroe joins Tristan Hughes at the British Museum to explore the Kingdom of Kush, an African empire that once ruled Egypt and defied Rome.

title: Encore: National Parks | Playgrounds of the People | 5
summary_one_sentence: Mining tycoon Stephen Mather campaigns to establish the National Parks Service and transform neglected parks into accessible public destinations, despite personal struggles.

title: Roaring Twenties | How the Money Rolls In | 3
summary_one_sentence: Harding's scandal-ridden presidency and the Teapot Dome affair give way to the Roaring Twenties boom, with Ford and GM's advertising rivalry defining mass consumerism.

title: The Roman Gladiator
summary_one_sentence: Gladiator expert Alexander Mariotti reveals the true origins, lifestyle, training, and mortality rates of Roman gladiators with host Tristan Hughes.

title: The Mayflower | The Thanksgiving Myth | 5
summary_one_sentence: Historian David Silverman reframes the Mayflower's 1620 arrival through the Wampanoag perspective, challenging the traditional Thanksgiving narrative.

title: Sex Work in Pompeii
summary_one_sentence: Sex historian Dr Kate Lister joins Tristan Hughes to examine the archaeology of Pompeii's brothels, erotic frescoes, and the lives of sex workers in ancient Rome.

title: Transcontinental Railroad | Work of Giants | 1
summary_one_sentence: Engineer Theodore Judah begins the decade-long effort to build America's first transcontinental railroad across nearly 2,000 miles of challenging terrain.

title: Alexander the Great | Rise to Power
summary_one_sentence: Dr Adrian Goldsworthy joins Tristan Hughes to trace Alexander the Great's formative years under Aristotle through his early battles and rise to Macedonian kingship.

title: Prehistoric Ireland: Newgrange
summary_one_sentence: Dr Muiris O'Sullivan joins Tristan Hughes to explore Newgrange, Ireland's 5,000-year-old Stone Age tomb, its construction, rock art, and DNA links to other Stone Age communities.

title: The Olmec: Mother of Mesoamerican Civilisations
summary_one_sentence: Dr Jillian Mollenhauer joins Tristan Hughes to examine Olmec art, symbolism, and how this foundational Mesoamerican civilization influenced the Maya and Aztec.

title: 121: Ed
summary_one_sentence: Penetration tester Ed Skoudis shares stories from his career, with additional commentary from Beau Woods of I am The Cavalry.
notes: Penetration testing is authorized work; classified as cybersecurity not cybercrime.

title: 60: dawgyg
summary_one_sentence: The story of hacker dawgyg and how he earned over $100,000 in a single day through hacking, likely bug bounties.
notes: Description is sparse; 'made money from hacking in a single day' could be bug bounty (cybersecurity) or cybercrime. Given Darknet Diaries framing and large sum, leaning cybersecurity (bug bounty) as the more common context for celebrated hacker stories.

title: 160: Greg
summary_one_sentence: Greg Linares, known as Laughing Mantis, recounts becoming the youngest hacker arrested in Arizona.
notes: Arrest indicates unauthorized access; classified as cybercrime primary.

title: 105: Secret Cells
summary_one_sentence: Journalist Joseph Cox of Motherboard discusses the underground world of encrypted phones used by criminals to evade law enforcement.
notes: Encrypted phones in Darknet Diaries context typically refers to criminal markets like EncroChat; classified cybercrime primary with surveillance_and_privacy secondary.

title: 111: ZeuS
summary_one_sentence: The ZeuS banking trojan, built to steal money from online bank accounts, grew into such a massive operation that it triggered one of the FBI's largest ever cybercrime investigations.

title: 82: Master of Pwn
summary_one_sentence: The Zero Day Initiative's Pwn2Own contest brings elite hackers together to find vulnerabilities in browsers, phones, and cars, with the top scorer crowned Master of Pwn.
notes: Authorized vulnerability contest; classified as cybersecurity.

title: 79: Dark Basin
summary_one_sentence: Citizen Lab investigates a massive hack-for-hire phishing campaign targeting victims who are being physically surveilled, tracing the attackers to bring them to justice.

title: 103: Cloud Hopper
summary_one_sentence: Incident responder Fabio Viggiani recounts investigating a major breach of one of his clients, likely connected to the Cloud Hopper nation-state espionage campaign.
notes: Cloud Hopper is a known nation-state espionage operation; breach investigation framed from defender side but underlying act is unauthorized access for gain/espionage, classified cybercrime primary.

title: 148: Dubsnatch
summary_one_sentence: Teenagers hack their way into unreleased dubstep music to get early access, in an audacious but low-stakes cybercrime story.

title: 113: Adam
summary_one_sentence: An IT worker at a learning academy finds himself pulled into a troubling series of events that derail his career and happiness.
notes: Description extremely sparse and ambiguous; classified cybercrime as default for Darknet Diaries non-security-professional narratives, but another labeler could reasonably differ.
